title: Authentication, Sessions, Passwords, Bearer Tokens, and Credential Recovery · Independent Go Trial
summary: An independent service team receives a different API, load, trust, persistence, and failure constraint and must transfer authentication, sessions, passwords, bearer tokens, and credential recovery into a new authentication and credential lifecycle policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of authentication and credential lifecycle policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Separate presented credential, verification, authenticated principal, session, assurance, freshness, and downstream authorization.
Use reviewed password hashing, bounded comparison, generic failures, rate and abuse controls, MFA or step-up policy, and secure recovery lifecycle.
Issue opaque sessions with rotation, server-side state or revocation, Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, Path, Domain, expiry, fixation defense, and logout invalidation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Commit to the mission model · Lab Authn Proof Principal Session
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab team handles case 9125 in a partner API with an outdated generated client. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate token claims and session rotation before constructing a principal; then make specification and runtime disagree on one nullable field and block release. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate presented credential, verification, authenticated principal, session, assurance, freshness, and downstream authorization.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Go server Commit to the mission model · Lab Authn Proof Principal Session.

For Go server Commit to the mission model · Lab Authn Proof Principal Session, keep Authn Proof Principal Session connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Oauth Resource Server Boundary condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Oauth Resource Server Boundary happy path, omit method, authority, credential, byte, time, redirect, retry, and cleanup boundaries, then accept plausible output.

steps.title: Go server Defend the transferred model · Lab Oauth Resource Server Boundary
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Oauth Resource Server Boundary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab team handles case 9134 in a canary service revision draining long-lived streams. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate token claims and session rotation before constructing a principal; then repeat the request with a different method and retain status plus durable-effect evidence. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat bearer access tokens as scoped resource-server credentials under RFC 6750 and BCP 9700 without implementing an authorization server casually.

Evidence target: Go server Defend the transferred model · Lab Oauth Resource Server Boundary.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Go server Defend the transferred model · Lab Oauth Resource Server Boundary, keep Oauth Resource Server Boundary connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Treat bearer access tokens as scoped resource-server credentials under RFC 6750 and BCP 9700 without implementing an authorization server casually.

hints: For Go server Defend the transferred model · Lab Oauth Resource Server Boundary, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Oauth Resource Server Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Go server Defend the transferred model · Lab Oauth Resource Server Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A lab team handles case 9134 in a canary service revision draining long-lived streams. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate token claims and session rotation before constructing a principal; then repeat the request with a different method and retain status plus durable-effect evidence. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat bearer access tokens as scoped resource-server credentials under RFC 6750 and BCP 9700 without implementing an authorization server casually.

For Go server Defend the transferred model · Lab Oauth Resource Server Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: OAuth access tokens are login sessions and an API key is equivalent to user authentication.
